item.title: NASSA Group workers block Tejgaon road for dues, trigger tailback
item.description: Vehicular movement remained suspended for about two and a half hours on Tuesday morning as workers of NASSA Group blocked Love Road at Tejgaon in the capital, demanding unpaid wages, causing severe traffic congestion and suffering for commuters....

item.title: Talks on final US deal to begin this week: Iran
item.description: Iran on Tuesday said talks with the United States on its nuclear programme and sanctions relief would likely begin later this week, as president Donald Trump’s announcement that the Strait of Hormuz would reopen sent oil prices tumbling, reports Agence France-Presse....

item.title: ​S Alam-linked hotels draw attention in Malaysia
item.description: Two prominent hotels in Kuala Lumpur linked to the business empire of S Alam Group chairman Mohammed Saiful Alam, widely known as S Alam, have come under increased scrutiny as international investigations into his alleged money-laundering activities expand across multiple jurisdictions....

item.title: BB plans tougher steps as bad loan recovery elusive
item.description: The Bangladesh Bank has intensified efforts to contain the country’s mounting default loan crisis through a series of regulatory, legal and policy measures, but economic weakness, lengthy legal processes and poor recovery rates continue to pose major obstacles to meaningful improvement......

item.title: BGB foils two more push-in bids
item.description: The Border Guard Bangladesh foiled two more push-in attempts by the Indian Border Security Force along Chapainawabganj and Jalampur on Monday night....

item.title: FIFA World Cup asks Bangladeshi fans if Argentina can win fourth title
item.description: The official Facebook page of the FIFA World Cup on Tuesday engaged Bangladeshi football fans with a post in Bengali, asking whether Argentina could add a fourth star to their jersey by winning the 2026 FIFA World Cup....

item.title: RMG exports to EU record steepest fall, shrink 19.4pc
item.description: Bangladesh suffered the largest decline among major apparel-exporting nations supplying the European Union during the first four months of 2026, as weakening demand and intensifying competition reshaped sourcing patterns on the bloc’s garment market......

item.title: Fitch flags execution risk in FY27 revenue targets
item.description: Bangladesh’s revenue mobilisation targets for the upcoming financial year of 2026-27 might be difficult to achieve, given the country’s weak record on tax collection and reform implementation, said the Fitch Ratings......

item.title: Concerns over relaxation of value-addition condition
item.description: Entrepreneurs in the textile, accessories and packaging sectors have expressed concern over the proposed removal of the minimum local value-addition requirement for non-bonded manufacturers that use duty-free imported raw materials under bank guarantees......

item.title: Orange summit eyes $100m climate fund
item.description: Bangladesh Bank deputy governor Habibur Rahman on Tuesday said innovative financial instruments like Orange Bonds can play a transformative role in advancing climate resilience, women’s empowerment and inclusive growth......

item.title: US envoy visits Rajshahi mango market
item.description: United States ambassador to Bangladesh Brent T Christensen on Tuesday visited the renowned Baneshwar Mango Market in Puthia upazila of Rajshahi, expressing admiration for the quality and taste of the region’s famous mangoes while underscoring the importance of strengthening Bangladesh’s cold-chain infrastructure to boost exports......

item.title: Brent oil price drops under $80 on US-Iran deal
item.description: Oil prices fell to a three-month low Tuesday on optimism over the expected reopening of the Strait of Hormuz after a peace deal between the United States and Iran......

item.title: Bangladesh wins 6 awards at China-South Asia expo
item.description: Bangladesh secured six prestigious awards at the ‘10th China-South Asia Expo’ and the ‘30th China Kunming Import-Export Fair 2026’, marking a significant achievement as the international trade event that concluded in Kunming on Tuesday......

item.title: Bangladesh showcases RMG products at Sydney expo
item.description: Bangladesh has showcased its ready-made garment products and export potential at the Global Sourcing Expo 2026 in Sydney, seeking to expand trade opportunities and strengthen commercial ties with Australia......

item.title: ACC sends docs to home ministry to bring back Benazir
item.description: The Anti-Corruption Commission on Tuesday sent the necessary documents to the home affairs ministry through the National Central Bureau, Bangladesh, to facilitate the return of former inspector general of police Benazir Ahmed, who was arrested in the United Arab Emirates....

item.title: Sonali Bank signs deal with Bangladesh Competition Commission
item.description: Sonali Bank PLC on Tuesday signed an agreement with Bangladesh Competition Commission to collect various charges-fees for filing online complaints through the Sonali Payment Gateway, said a press release......

item.title: Dutch-Bangla Bank approves 30pc dividends
item.description: Shareholders of Dutch-Bangla Bank PLC on Tuesday approved 25 per cent cash dividend and 5 per cent stock dividend for the year 2025, said a press release......

item.title: AIB holds workshop on investment risk management
item.description: Al-Arafah Islami Bank on Tuesday began a three-day training course titled ‘Investment Risk Management’ at its training institute, said a press release......

item.title: BRAC Bank, CCI Bangladesh partner to boost distribution
item.description: BRAC Bank signed a distributor finance agreement with Coca-Cola İçecek Bangladesh to strengthen its distribution ecosystem, said a press release......

item.title: Janata Bank signs deal with Farazy Hospital
item.description: Janata Bank PLC on Monday signed a corporate agreement regarding medical service with Farazy Hospital Limited, said a press release......

item.title: Pubali Bank okays 30pc dividends
item.description: Shareholders of Pubali Bank PLC on Tuesday approved 10 per cent cash dividend and 20 per cent stock dividend for the year ended December 31, 2025, said a press release......

item.title: Bank Asia, CPDL to facilitate home loan services
item.description: Bank Asia PLC and CA Property Development Limited signed an agreement to provide convenient and attractive home loan facilities to prospective apartment buyers of CPDL projects. The partnership will also include joint marketing initiatives, customer engagement programmes and promotional activities, said a press release......

item.title: DANO Daily Pushti launches special football fan pack
item.description: On the occasion of FIFA World Cup 2026, Arla’s DANO Daily Pushti has launched four special fun packs, said a press release......

item.title: ACC at risk of becoming a bureaucratic appendage
item.description: THE draft amendment to the Anti-Corruption Commission Act 2004 raises serious concerns about the autonomy and the future of the country’s anti-corruption watchdog. Instead of strengthening an institution that has for long struggled with political interference, the proposed changes appear designed to weaken its independence further and place it under greater......

item.title: Social safety initiatives call for transparent evaluation
item.description: THE government’s decision to assess the impact of the Family Card programme on beneficiaries’ living standards is a necessary step to ensure that a major public welfare initiative delivers its intended outcomes. Officials at a review meeting on June 15 said that surveys would be conducted to determine how far the cash transfer scheme has improved the health, education......

item.title: Proposed ACC amendments betray spirit of reform
item.description: THE suggested modifications to the Anti-Corruption Commission Act are creating significant concerns regarding the future ability of the anti-corruption agency of the nation to operate independently. While some suggestions are made with the purpose of widening the jurisdiction of the commission, others have the potential to compromise institutional safeguards......

item.title: Powering irrigation beyond fossil fuels
item.description: BANGLADESH’S agricultural future will be determined not only by how much food it can produce but also by how it powers that production. For decades, discussions on food security have focused primarily on seeds, fertilisers, irrigation coverage and crop yields. Energy, though central to modern agriculture, has often remained a secondary concern. Yet as fuel prices......

item.title: In light of Bengal’s anticolonial struggles
item.description: IN 2008, during my tenure with the University of Dhaka, I had a three-month postdoctoral fellowship at the Forum on Contemporary Theory in Baroda, India. I made friends with some Indian researchers there. Once I asked one of them about major writers in India. Most of the names they mentioned in response to my question were from Bengal. I wondered and asked......

item.title: Cyber range platform built with own resources
item.description: In today’s hyperconnected world, cybersecurity has evolved from a technical concern into a core element of national security and strategic capability. Nations are no longer judged solely by economic or military strength, but also by their ability to defend digital infrastructure, protect sensitive data and respond effectively to cyber threats. As governments, financial......

item.title: Neymar back in training with Brazil
item.description: Brazil forward Neymar, who was diagnosed with a right calf injury in late May, trained at the World Cup for the first time Tuesday in a sign he may be nearing a return....

item.title: France and two-goal Mbappe roar into World Cup
item.description: Kylian Mbappe scored twice as France launched their bid for a third World Cup crown with a 3-1 victory over Senegal in Group I on Tuesday....

item.title: Iran threatens ‘harsh response’ to Israeli attacks in Lebanon
item.description: Iran’s military threatened on Tuesday to respond to Israel after strikes in southern Lebanon killed four people despite a deal between Tehran and Washington ending the war......

item.title: Govt hikes online seminar allowances for officials
item.description: The government on Tuesday raised the online seminar allowances ranging from 80 per cent to 300 per cent for its officials and employees....

item.title: AL brings out flash procession in Sylhet, 4 held
item.description: A group of the leaders and activists of the Awami League and its affiliated organisations, whose activities are currently banned, brought out a flash procession in Sylhet on Tuesday early morning....

item.title: Fakhrul calls for unity to protect press freedom
item.description: LGRD and co-operatives minister Mirza Fakhrul Islam Alamgir on Tuesday called on all those who struggled for democracy and press freedom to remain united to protect media independence....

item.title: G7 powers push Russia to end Ukraine war
item.description: G7 leaders agreed on Tuesday to intensify pressure on Russia to end more than four years of war against Ukraine, with US president Donald Trump saying Moscow should ‘make a deal’....

item.title: 2.4m refugees need resettling in 2027: UN
item.description: Almost 2.4 million refugees will need resettling next year, the United Nations said on Tuesday, at a time when a number of countries have slashed resettlement spots....
